59.reason: [Loop only checks EOF; if the reader is at end-of-line and addToken() makes no progress, tokenization can stall or loop incorrectly.]

89.reason: [If the current line has no remaining characters, returning without advancing the reader causes tokenize() to repeatedly process the same position.]

120.reason: No advancement or error handling when no token pattern matches; causes infinite loop if input contains unrecognized character.

reasons.buggy-claude-sonnet-4-6-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If no token type matches, this method also returns without advancing position, which can lead to an infinite loop on unexpected input.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  No advancement or error handling when no token pattern matches; causes infinite loop if input contains unrecognized character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  If no token pattern matches, the reader position is not advanced, causing an infinite loop in tokenize(). Unrecognized characters must be consumed or reported.

202.reason: [Bug: Assumes every line ends with a newline. When the last line has no trailing newline, this subtracts an extra 1 and incorrectly advances past the end of file, causing position tracking errors.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Bug: Assumes every line ends with a newline. When the last line has no trailing newline, this subtracts an extra 1 and incorrectly advances past the end of file, causing position tracking errors.]

198.reason: The condition should be `steps <= remainingInLine`. When `steps` exactly equals the number of remaining characters in the current line, the code incorrectly enters the `else` branch, prematurely advancing to the next line and causing incorrect line number tracking and potential character skipping.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition should be `steps <= remainingInLine`. When `steps` exactly equals the number of remaining characters in the current line, the code incorrectly enters the `else` branch, prematurely advancing to the next line and causing incorrect line number tracking and potential character skipping.
